nodes.name: Tawny-shouldered blackbird
nodes.summary: Tawny-shouldered blackbird（Agelaius humeralis）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如Tawny-shouldered blackbird。

nodes.name: Tawny-shouldered blackbird
nodes.summary: The tawny-shouldered blackbird (Agelaius humeralis), known in Spanish as mayito, is a species of bird in the family Icteridae. It is found in Cuba and Hispaniola (split between the Dominican Republic and Haiti). It is a vagrant in the United States (to the Florida Keys). Description Measuring 20 cm (7.9 in) long, this highly social species is entirely black, save for the namesake brown-orange patch at the shoulder. The patch may not be visible when the wings are folded. Taxonomy Two subspecies are described: A. h. humeralis – (Vigors, 1827): nominate, found in Cuba and Hispaniola A. h. scopulus – Garrido, 1970: found on Cayo Cantiles (east of Isla de la Juventud off southwestern Cuba) Breeding They breed from April to August, laying 3–4 greenish-white eggs spotted with brown in a cup-shaped nest that is lined with soft materials and placed in a tree. Diet and habitat Tawny-shouldered blackbirds eat insects, seeds, nectar, fruit, and small lizards. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry shrubland, pastureland, and heavily degraded former forest. References External links BirdLife species factsheet for Agelaius humeralis "Agelaius humeralis". Avibase. "Tawny-shouldered blackbird media". Internet Bird Collection. Tawny-shouldered blackbird photo gallery at VIREO (Drexel University) Tawny-shouldered blackbird species account at Neotropical Birds (Cornell Lab of Ornithology) Audio recordings of Tawny-shouldered blackbird on Xeno-canto. See also Red-shouldered blackbird (mayito de la ciénaga)
